text: Tell me about yourself and what drew you to this role.
Why this company, and why now?
Walk me through a project you’re proud of. What was your contribution?
Describe a time you faced a challenge and how you handled it.
Tell me about a time you had to learn something quickly.
Describe a time you made a mistake. What did you do next?
Tell me about a time you received feedback. How did you respond?
Describe a time you worked with a difficult teammate. What did you do?
How do you prioritize tasks when everything feels urgent?
Tell me about a time you took initiative without being asked.
Tell me about a time you improved a process or workflow.
Describe a time you had to manage competing priorities.
Tell me about a time you influenced a decision.
Describe a time you handled ambiguity. What did you do first?
Tell me about a time you had to communicate bad news.
Describe a time you disagreed with someone. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a time you balanced speed vs. quality.
Describe a time you mentored or helped someone succeed.
Tell me about a time you worked under pressure.
Tell me about a time you had to manage stakeholder expectations.
What motivates you to do your best work?
What are your strengths? Give a quick example.
What’s a weakness you’re actively improving? How?
Describe a time you had to collaborate across different roles (design/product/QA/etc.).
Tell me about a time you had to speak up or advocate for something.
Describe a time you had limited time and still delivered results.
Tell me about a time you handled a conflict professionally.
Describe how you plan your work when starting a new project.
Tell me about a time you used data or evidence to support a decision.
Describe a time you went above and beyond expectations.
Describe a time you owned a deliverable end-to-end. What did you learn?
Tell me about a time you improved performance or reliability.
Describe a time you had to debug a tough issue. What was your approach?
Tell me about a time you had to push back on a request.
Describe a time you reduced risk (testing, monitoring, rollback plan, etc.).
Tell me about a time you handled a production incident (or simulated one).
Describe a time you improved developer experience or documentation.
Tell me about a time you delivered with incomplete information.
Describe a time you introduced a new tool or method. How did you get buy-in?
Tell me about a time you handled multiple stakeholders with different priorities.
Why are you interested in this field?
What kind of team environment helps you thrive?
What do you want to learn during this internship?
What does success look like for you in the next 6–12 months?
What criteria do you use to decide between job offers?
What kind of problems energize you the most?
What’s a product you love and why?
If you could improve one thing about your last project, what would it be?
How do you keep yourself motivated during repetitive tasks?
What kind of mentorship are you looking for?
Explain what an API is in simple terms.
What is the difference between HTTP GET and POST?
What does it mean for code to be 'idempotent'?
What is a database index and why is it useful?
Explain the difference between synchronous and asynchronous code.
What is the event loop (high level)?
What is CORS and why does it exist?
What is authentication vs authorization?
Explain cookies vs localStorage vs sessionStorage.
What is SQL injection and how do you prevent it?
Explain REST and what makes a good RESTful API design.
Compare REST vs GraphQL. When would you choose each?
What is caching? Where would you add it in a web app?
How do you diagnose a slow endpoint in production?
Explain how you would design a rate limiter.
How do you ensure code quality when shipping quickly?
Explain eventual consistency in distributed systems (high level).
What are common reasons a Node/Express server crashes?
How would you structure logs/metrics for a backend service?
How do you handle secrets (API keys) safely in a project?
What is Git rebase vs merge (high level)?
What are unit tests and why are they useful?
Explain what a 'CI pipeline' does.
What is a 404 vs a 500 error?
What does 'responsive design' mean and how do you achieve it?
Explain a complex topic you know well to a non-technical person.
How do you make sure you understood a requirement correctly?
How do you handle unclear requirements from stakeholders?
How do you write updates so stakeholders stay confident?
How do you ask for help without wasting other people’s time?
How would you improve a product you use every day?
How do you measure whether a feature is successful?
Describe a time you noticed a user problem and proposed a solution.
How do you decide what to build first when time is limited?
What’s one user experience mistake you see often? How would you fix it?
Tell me about a time you dealt with stress before a deadline.
Describe a time you had to present or explain your work.
Tell me about a time you improved your own skills outside of class/work.
Describe a time you handled a repetitive task efficiently.
Tell me about a time you supported a teammate who was struggling.
Tell me about a time you simplified something complex.
Describe a time you improved system reliability or reduced incidents.
Tell me about a time you had to estimate work and you were wrong. What changed?
Describe a time you made a trade-off decision. What did you prioritize?
Tell me about a time you delivered a feature end-to-end with testing.
What is a promise in JavaScript and why is it useful?
Explain map/filter/reduce with a simple example.
What’s the difference between var, let, and const?
What does 'immutable' mean in programming?
Explain what a CDN does.
How would you design an API endpoint for uploading files safely?
Explain optimistic vs pessimistic locking.
How do you prevent duplicated requests from creating duplicated data?
How would you design a pagination strategy for large datasets?
What steps do you take to secure an Express API?
How do you adapt your communication style to different people?
Tell me about a time you aligned multiple people to a plan.
How do you handle a situation where someone misunderstands your message?
How do you make sure you’re listening effectively?
Tell me about a time you asked a great question that changed the outcome.
Pick a feature in a popular app and describe how you'd improve it.
How would you A/B test a new onboarding flow?
How do you decide what matters most to users?
How do you handle technical debt while still shipping new work?
